Western blot protocol for adherent cells

Lysis buffer with protease and phosphatase inhibitor
- 10 ml lysis buffer
- 1 protease and phosphatase tablet
- Vortex to mix and dissolve

1. Place culture dish on ice
2. Aspirate media
3. Wash cells with PBS (5 ml for 10 ml or 1 ml for 6-well)
4. Aspirate PBS
5. Add PBS to dish
6. Scrap cells off dish and transfer cells into a conical vial
7. Centrifuge @ ~14-17,000 g for 20 min in 4C
8. Remove supernatent
9. Re-suspend cells in ~1.5ml PBS and transfer into a microcentrifuge tube
10. Centrifuge @ ~14-17,000 g for 20 min in 4C
11. Resuspend cells in 500 ul of lysis buffer 
12. Sonicate cells for 10-15 seconds @ 4C
13. Centrifuge @ ~14-17,000 g for 20 min in 4C
14. Transfer supernantent to a fresh microcentrifuge tube without disturbing the pellet. Discard pellet.
15. Determine protein concentration using BCA protein assay
16. Add 50ul of 10x SDS sample buffer into lysate to a final concentration of 1x 
17. Denature proteins by boiling @ 95-100C for 5 min
18. Load 1-50 ug of protein onto SDS-page well. Amount of protein to load depends on protein abundance and how sensitive the antibody is. 

 -->
